data.single_linear: Exploring research in Explainable AI and Adversarial Machine Learning for Trustworthy AI, with 4+ years of industry experience.
data.about_me:: I am a Computer Science researcher and incoming PhD student with a focus on Explainable AI (XAI) and Adversarial Machine Learning. My work explores building trustworthy and interpretable AI systems, with publications at venues including NeurIPS, AAAI and IEEE JBHI. Prior to research, I spent 4+ years as a Software Engineer, bringing a strong foundation in building scalable, production-grade applications.
data.research_vision: My research is driven by a single question: how do we make AI systems worthy of trust? At a time when AI is reshaping every critical domain — healthcare, law, finance, national security — the cost of opaque, brittle, and vulnerable models is no longer theoretical. My work sits at the intersection of Explainable AI, Adversarial Robustness, and AI Security, with a singular goal: to make intelligent systems not just powerful, but accountable, resilient, and safe for the communities that depend on them. I believe responsible AI is not a constraint on progress — it is the condition for it.</br>Before research, I spent 4+ years as a Software Engineer building production-grade systems used by thousands — across telemedicine platforms in South Asia and international tax research tools used across Europe. That background shapes how I think: not just as a scientist asking what is possible, but as an engineer who knows what it takes to build things that must actually work in the world.
